mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly higher during FEDFUNDS declines (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% YoY signals central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive despite high inflation (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals robust supply-side expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Either INDPRO or GDPC1 fails to show growth, leading to negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive during tightening periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth (PAYEMS) signals a healthy labor market, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are not significantly higher during PAYEMS growth (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Either RSAFS or HOUST fails to show growth, resulting in negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment rates (UNRATE) signal economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive despite high unemployment (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weak supply-side activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive despite INDPRO decline (Rank IC < 0.03).
